mnemonics: TEST: T-Try, E-Every, S-Scenario, T-Thoroughly. This helps remember that testing in Human-Centred AI requires exhaustive evaluation of all user scenarios to ensure the AI works as intended for real people.
keyPoints: The test component involves evaluating the AI system with real users in realistic scenarios.
Its purpose is to validate that the system meets user needs and requirements, ensuring it is useful and usable.
Testing is an iterative process, often involving prototypes, to identify and fix issues before full deployment.
commonMistakes: Testing the system only with developers or technical staff, rather than with a diverse group of actual end-users.
Focusing solely on technical performance metrics (e.g., accuracy, speed) while neglecting human-centric metrics like user satisfaction, trust, and ease of use.
